channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ex-officer on trial in shooting death of Andre Hill thought his life was in danger, attorney says
item.description: An attorney for a white former Ohio police officer says the officer mistakenly shot and killed Andre Hill in 2002 because he saw a flash of light from keys in the Black man’s hand and thought he was about to be shot.
item.dc:creator: John Seewer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: McDonald's E. coli outbreak: Why only the Quarter Pounder was linked
item.description: McDonald's has voluntarily stopped selling its Quarter Pounder hamburger at restaurants across Colorado and other states after an E. coli outbreak was linked to the menu item.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, KRON4

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day on Thursday following an attack on the premises of a key defense company that killed at least five people, the state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutors are set to reveal whether they favor resentencing Erik and Lyle Menendez
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors will announce Thursday whether or not they recommend resentencing Erik and Lyle Menendez for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills. The brothers were sentenced in 1996 to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia officials say they thwarted an attempt to crash a state election website
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia election officials acted quickly earlier this month to thwart an attempt to flood the state's absentee voter portal in an apparent attempt to crash the site, the secretary of state's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: World on pace for significantly more warming without immediate climate action, report warns
item.description: The world is on a path to get 1.8 degrees Celsius (3.2 Fahrenheit) warmer than it is now, a United Nations report said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: The partial collapse of a ferry dock that left seven people dead during an annual cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Russ Bynum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida sues Garland for blocking Trump assassination attempt investigation
item.description: Florida sued Attorney General Merrick Garland on Wednesday, saying the Justice Department is improperly blocking the state from investigating the second assassination attempt of former President Trump. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas teen arrested 3 months after Sonic manager shot and killed over fake money payment
item.description: A Texas teen accused of shooting, killing a San Antonio Sonic manager after trying to pay for food with counterfeit money was caught more than three months later in Dallas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — An Arizona prosecutor said the man arrested in the shooting of a Democratic National Committee office in suburban Phoenix had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home, leading law enforcement to believe he may have been planning a mass casualty event.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW and WALTER BERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: The Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump assassination attempt: Secret Service knew airspace protection would end with former president onstage
item.description: Additional lapses in public airspace restrictions on the day of former President Trump's July 13 rally continues to raise questions and concerns about security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tim Burton talks about his dread of AI as an exhibition of his work opens in London
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The imagination of Tim Burton has produced ghosts and ghouls, Martians, monsters and misfits — all on display at an exhibition that is opening in London just in time for Halloween. But you know what really scares him? Artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boeing factory workers vote to reject contract and continue 6-week strike
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers voted Wednesday to reject the company’s latest contract offer and to continue a six-week strike that has halted production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG and MANUEL VALDES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The 2024 election is major source of stress: Survey
item.description: A total of 72% worry that the election results could lead to violence, and 56% believe the election could lead to the end of democracy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Kamala Harris wrong about Trump's Hitler comments made to Kelly
item.description: "They (Trump's comments) don't mean anything, and he doesn't even remember them 30 seconds later, O'Reilly said on "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Man allegedly asked for ‘rapiest frat’ at NC State before waving gun: warrant
item.description: A new search warrant reveals more details about a man arrested at North Carolina State University in September for carrying a gun on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former 'American Idol' hopeful arrested on child porn charges
item.description: Police say they found hundreds of images and videos of child pornography on a smartphone while searching his home.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Richardson/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina government calculates Hurricane Helene damages, needs at least $53B
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The catastrophic flooding and destruction caused by Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina likely caused at least a record $53 billion in damages and recovery needs, Gov. Roy Cooper's administration said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ronna McDaniel: How Republicans can win Gen Z voters
item.description: In an election that will be won on the margins, the failure of Republicans to engage these new young voters would amount to a missed opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Ronna McDaniel, opinion contributor, NewsNation

item.title: Laredo lifts boil water order after E. coli 'crisis' infiltrated city's water
item.description: he South Texas border city of Laredo has lifted its boil-water order after dangerous E. coli was found in the city's water system, which has been blamed on illegal or faulty water connections.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Political strategist James Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” James Carville said in an essay published by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: LIST: Waffles, pancakes recall expanded over potential listeria contamination
item.description: The recalled products were sold under multiple brand names across the entire country, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Asian American boba brand finds opportunity after Simu Liu sparks cultural appropriation debate
item.description: Olivia Chen and Pauline Ang, friends and business partners on boba milk tea brand Twrl, have tried three times to get on “Shark Tank,” the ABC reality show where up-and-coming entrepreneurs try to woo big-name backers.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico authorities rescue hundreds after flooding strands many in high water and leaves 2 dead
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A southeastern New Mexico community began to dry out Monday after historic rainfall over the weekend produced severe flooding that left at least two people dead and hundreds stranded on rooftops.
item.dc:creator: WALTER BERRY and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Former Iron Maiden singer Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name Paul Di'Anno, has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched this week by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9
